summary: The FOMC concludes today, March 18, with markets watching Powell's press conference for any signal on inflation given spiking energy prices tied to the escalating Middle East conflict. Futures pricing suggests the Fed won't ease until at least September, with only a single cut expected this year — keeping duration under pressure and gold's safe-haven bid capped by a rising DXY.
tweet: Fed day: Powell must navigate the Iran war, inflation fears, and a choppy labor market — while a post-IEEPA tariff rebuild keeps stagflation on the table across every asset class.
